task_completion.description: The agent navigated to and opened the Markups module in 3D Slicer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The left sidebar displays the 'Markups' module panel, or the Markups toolbar is clearly active and visible.

task_completion.description: Fiducial markers are visually placed on the tumor boundaries in the slice views.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the slice views (Red, Yellow, or Green windows), colored marker dots or crosshairs are visible specifically on or at the edges of the bright (hyperintense) tumor region.

task_completion.description: The Markups list shows exactly 4 points created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The table in the Markups module control panel lists exactly 4 rows/items.
The 'Name' column in the Markups table is visible and contains the specific labels: 'Superior', 'Inferior', 'Anterior', and 'Posterior'.

task_completion.description: The agent saved the markup list to the correct filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save Data' dialog or Markups save interface is visible, showing the filename 'tumor_boundaries.mrk.json' being saved.

integrity.description: The agent did not use the Python Interactor to programmatically place markers.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots do not show code being typed into the Python console to add markups or set coordinates.

integrity.description: The agent used the Slicer GUI to place points manually.
integrity.visual_evidence: The mouse cursor is visible interacting with the slice views (placing points) or the Markups panel buttons.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply copy-paste a pre-made solution file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is not seen using the system file explorer or terminal to copy a file to the destination without interacting with the Slicer application.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires placing exactly 4 fiducial markers named 'Superior', 'Inferior', 'Anterior', and 'Posterior'. The tumor appears as a bright (hyperintense) region on the MRI slices. The output file must be named 'tumor_boundaries.mrk.json'.
